question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: Based on search results, multiple sources indicate the March 2026 CPI actually increased 0.3% month-over-month, which is significantly below the 0.8% threshold needed for YES resolution. <cite index="5-7">The BLS report shows the Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) increased 0.3% in March on a seasonally adjusted basis.</cite> This was despite earlier forecasts predicting much higher inflation due to energy price spikes from the Iran conflict. <cite index="14-17">Headline CPI is expected to be up 0.8% from February to March and 3.1% from the year prior, according to FactSet.</cite> The actual result came in well below these expectations.
key_factors: March 2026 CPI reported as 0.3% monthly increase, well below 0.8% threshold
Energy price impact from Iran conflict was less severe than initially feared
Market appears to be pricing in pre-release forecasts rather than actual data
Core CPI showed contained inflation pressures despite energy concerns
risks: Potential data revisions could change the official figure
Market may have access to information not reflected in search results
Resolution criteria interpretation could differ from standard CPI reporting
Timing confusion between forecast expectations and actual reported data
